prediction_metadata.raw_prompt:
You are selecting arguments for a single MCP tool call.
Return valid JSON only with a single top-level object named `arguments`.
Use only fields allowed by the schema.

Tool name: read_text_file
Tool description: Read the complete contents of a file from the file system as text. Handles various text encodings and provides detailed error messages if the file cannot be read. Use this tool when you need to examine the contents of a single file. Use the head parameter to read only the first N lines of a file, or the tail parameter to read only the last N lines of a file. Operates on the file as text regardless of extension. Only works within allowed directories.
Skill condition: multi_candidate_skill
Skill summary: Read the complete contents of a file from the file system as text. Handles various text encodings and provides detailed error messages if the file cannot be read. Use this tool when you need to examine the contents of a.
When to use: ["Use `read_text_file` when the user's request directly matches this tool's purpose.", "Provide the required field `path`."]
When not to use: ["Do not call this tool when required inputs are missing or ambiguous.", "Do not invent unsupported parameters or unsupported enum values.", "Do not use when required inputs are missing."]
Argument template: {"path": "data/sample.txt", "tail": 1.0, "head": 1.0}
Examples: [{"scenario": "Minimal valid request that satisfies the required fields for read_text_file", "arguments": {"path": "data/sample.txt"}}, {"scenario": "Richer invocation that uses optional controls for read_text_file", "arguments": {"path": "data/sample.txt", "tail": 2.0, "head": 2.0}}]
Schema: {"properties": {"head": {"description": "If provided, returns only the first N lines of the file.", "type": "number"}, "path": {"description": "Path to the file within an allowed directory.", "type": "string"}, "tail": {"description": "If provided, returns only the last N lines of the file.", "type": "number"}}, "required": ["path"], "type": "object"}
